zh.Rostów, Wałbrzych, Poland: 波蘭沃也奧特沃 瓦武布日斯吉洛斯托福
zh.lifeSummary: 當Dmitrij Borisovich, Duke Of Rostov And Uglich於1253年9月11日出生在俄羅斯Ярославская областьРостов時，他的父親Yaroslav Vasilievich22歲，母親Princess of Murom Mariya Yaroslavna22歲。他和Mrs. Dmitrij Borisovich Duchess Of Rostov至少有1個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1293年享年40歲，埋葬在波蘭沃也奧特沃 瓦武布日斯吉洛斯托福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Dmitrij Borisovich, Duke Of Rostov And Uglich于1253年9月11日出生在俄罗斯Ярославская областьРостов时，他的父亲Yaroslav Vasilievich22岁，母亲Princess of Murom Mariya Yaroslavna22岁。他和Mrs. Dmitrij Borisovich Duchess Of Rostov至少有1个儿子和3个女儿。他去世于1293年享年40岁，埋葬在波兰沃也奧特沃 瓦武布日斯吉洛斯托福。

nameMeanings.name: Rostov
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Bulgarian and Macedonian: patronymic from a short form of the personal name Kostadin, an equivalent of Constantine . Compare Kostoff .

variants.name: Kostoff
meanings: Westernized form of Bulgarian and Macedonian   Kostov  .

variants.name: Constantine
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Constantin, Costantin (from Latin Constantinus, a derivative of constans ‘steadfast’). The name was popular in Continental Europe, and to a lesser extent in England, as having been borne by the first Christian ruler of the Roman Empire, Constantine the Great (c. 280–337), in whose honor Byzantium was renamed Constantinople. In some cases the name may be an Americanized form of one of the many cognates in other languages, in particular Greek Konstantinos.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Cotentin (Coutances) in La Manche, France (see   Constance   2).

nameMeanings.name: And
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Isidor, Myer.

English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a maker of hoops and bands, etc., from Middle English band, bond, Middle High German, Middle Low German bant, German Band ‘hoop, metal band, fetter, shackle’, i.e. something used for tying or binding.
English: variant of   Bond  .

variants.name: Bandt
meanings: German: variant of   Band  .

variants.name: Bandstra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Wouter.
West Frisian: habitational name composed with Band 4 + the suffix -stra from Old Frisian sittera ‘inhabitant of’.

variants.name: Banta
meanings: West Frisian: probably a habitational name for someone from Bant, in the 17th century an island in Friesland, now a village north of Emmeloord in the Noordoostpolder (see   Band   4, compare   Banda   5). This surname is no longer found in the Netherlands.
History: Epke Jacobs Banta arrived in New Netherland in 1659.

variants.name: Bandel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Zvi, Emanuel, Hyman, Meir.
South German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from a diminutive of   Band  . See also   Bendel  .
German: in some cases, a variant of Pandel, a pet form of the medieval personal name Pantaleon (see Pantaleo ).

variants.name: Bend
meanings: English (Leicestershire and Nottinghamshire): from Middle English bend ‘band, fetter, chain; ribbon, sash, stripe’, for someone who made, wore, or used such items. See also the Band 1 and compare Bendy 1.

variants.name: Bandler
meanings: German: occupational name for a maker of band (for vessels) or ribbon or lace, from an agent derivative of Middle High German bandel, a diminutive of bant ‘band’ (see Band ).

variants.name: Bandy
meanings: English: perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English bandi, bondi, bendi, derivatives of Middle English and Old English band, bond, bend ‘band, ribbon, stripe’ + Middle English -i, reinforced by Old French bendé ‘striped’, with reference perhaps to clothing ornamented with ribbons or stripes, or to the diagonal band of a heraldic device. Compare Band .
English: alternatively, perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English bandy, bendy ‘curved stick’, used for someone with bent or crooked legs.
Czech and Slovak: from a derivative of banda ‘(gipsy) band’, apparently a nickname for a bandsman. This surname is found among Gypsies.

variants.name: Bond
meanings: English: status name for a peasant farmer or husbandman, Middle English bond(e), bounde, occasionally bande ‘bondman, customary tenant, serf’ (Old English bonda, bunda, reinforced by Old Norse bóndi). The Old Norse word was also in use as a personal name (Old Norse Bóndi, Bondi, Bundi, Bonde, borrowed as late Old English Bonda), and this has given rise to other English and Scandinavian surnames alongside those originating as status names, such as the Middle English personal name Bonde. The status of the peasant farmer fluctuated considerably during the Middle Ages; moreover, the underlying ancient Germanic word is of disputed origin and meaning. Among ancient Germanic peoples who settled to an agricultural life, the term came to signify a farmer holding lands from, and bound by loyalty to, a lord; from this developed the sense of a free landholder as opposed to a serf. In England after the Norman Conquest the word sank in status and became associated with the notion of bound servitude. The name can also be a variant of Band .
Swedish: variant of   Bonde  .
In some cases also an American shortened form of Ukrainian Bondarenko and possibly also of some other surname beginning with Bond-.

nameMeanings.name: Uglich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Gottfried.
German (Saxony): variant of   Ulrich  .

variants.name: Ulrich
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Hans, Gerhard, Klaus, Frieda, Fritz, Reinhard, Alfons, Alois, Bernd.

German: from the personal name Ulrich, Old High German Odalrīc, composed of the elements odal ‘inherited property, fortune’ + rīc ‘power’. The name was borne by a 10th-century Christian saint, bishop of Augsburg, whose fame contributed greatly to the popularity of the personal name in German- and Slavic-speaking areas in the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: Dmitrij
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek Dēmētrios ‘devotee of Demeter’. See Demetrius .

variants.name: Demetrius
meanings: form of Greek Dēmētrios ‘follower of Demeter’, classical goddess of fertility. This name was borne by several early Christian martyrs; its huge popularity in eastern Europe is due in particular to the fame of a 4th-century saint martyred under Diocletian. For some reason, it never achieved much popularity in western Europe.

nameMeanings.name: Duke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: In modern use this normally represents a coinage parallel to   Earl   and   King  , but it has been used as a short form of   Marmaduke   from at least the early 1600s.

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

en.lifeSummary: When Dmitrij Borisovich, Duke Of Rostov And Uglich was born on 11 September 1253, in Rostova, Yaroslavl Oblast, Russia, his father, Yaroslav Vasilievich, was 22 and his mother, Princess of Murom Mariya Yaroslavna, was 22. He had at least 1 son and 3 daughters with Mrs. Dmitrij Borisovich Duchess Of Rostov. He died in 1293, at the age of 40, and was buried in Rostów, Wałbrzych Voivodeship, Poland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Dmitrij Borisovich, Duke Of Rostov And Uglich est né le 11 septembre 1253, à Rostova, Yaroslavskaya Oblast', Russie, son père, Yaroslav Vasilievich, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Princess of Murom Mariya Yaroslavna, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles avec Mrs. Dmitrij Borisovich Duchess Of Rostov. Il est décédé en 1293, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterré à Rostów, Województwo wałbrzyskie, Pologne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Dmitrij Borisovich, Duke Of Rostov And Uglich am 11. September 1253 in Rostova, Yaroslavskaya Oblast', Russland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Yaroslav Vasilievich 22 und seine Mutter Princess of Murom Mariya Yaroslavna 22. Er hatte mit Mrs. Dmitrij Borisovich Duchess Of Rostov mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1293 im Alter von 40 Jahren und wurde in Rostów, Województwo wałbrzyskie, Polen begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Dmitrij Borisovich, Duke Of Rostov And Uglich è nato il il 11 settembre 1253 nel Rostova, Yaroslavskaya Oblast', Russia, il suo padre, Yaroslav Vasilievich, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Princess of Murom Mariya Yaroslavna, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie con Mrs. Dmitrij Borisovich Duchess Of Rostov. Morì nel 1293, all'età di 40 anni, e fu sepolto a Rostów, Województwo wałbrzyskie, Polonia.

ja.Rostów, Wałbrzych, Poland: ポーランドウオジュoドズトウオ ワlブルズイスキロストoウ
ja.lifeSummary: Dmitrij Borisovich, Duke Of Rostov And Uglichが1253年9月11日にロシアЯрославская областьРостовで生まれた時，父Yaroslav Vasilievichは22歳，母Princess of Murom Mariya Yaroslavnaは22歳であった。彼はMrs. Dmitrij Borisovich Duchess Of Rostovとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1293年に40歳で死去し，ポーランドウオジュoドズトウオ ワlブルズイスキロストoウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Dmitrij Borisovich, Duke Of Rostov And Uglich(이)가 1253년 9월 11일 러시아 Ярославская область Ростов에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Yaroslav Vasilievich(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Princess of Murom Mariya Yaroslavna(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Mrs. Dmitrij Borisovich Duchess Of Rostov(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1293년 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 폴란드 보jwo드즈트보 와l브르즈이스키 로스트ow에 묻혔다.
